title: MD
biography: Ameyo Adadevoh was a Nigerian endocrinologist who single-handedly prevented a catastrophic Ebola outbreak in Lagos in 2014. She diagnosed the index case Patrick Sawyer, quarantined him against enormous pressure, and contained the virus, saving potentially millions of lives in Africa's most populous city. She contracted Ebola and died in the line of duty.
aiSummary: Ameyo Adadevoh was an endocrinology specialist at First Consultants Medical Centre, Lagos. Prevented Ebola outbreak in Nigeria in 2014 by quarantining the index case; died of the disease. Based in Lagos, Nigeria.
education: University of Lagos College of Medicine
awards: Grand Commander of the Order of the Niger (posthumous)

timeline.title: Medical degree from University of Lagos
timeline.description: Graduated from the University of Lagos College of Medicine

timeline.title: Lead consultant at First Consultants Medical Centre
timeline.description: Became the lead consultant endocrinologist at the private hospital in Lagos

timeline.title: Diagnosed and quarantined Nigeria's Ebola index case
timeline.description: Identified Patrick Sawyer as the first Ebola case in Nigeria and insisted on quarantine despite government pressure to release him

timeline.title: Died of Ebola contracted in the line of duty
timeline.description: Contracted Ebola while treating Sawyer and died on August 19, 2014, hailed as a national hero
